term: yaṁ papañceti tato nidānaṁ purisaṁ papañcasaññāsaṅkhā samudācaranti
preferred_translation: from what one proliferates about as the source, the recognitions and notions of proliferation sweep over a person
alternative_translations: from what one proliferates about, the recognitions and notions of proliferation sweep over a person
discouraged_translations: from conceptual proliferation, concepts arise in a person
definition: Culminating MN 18 sequence line showing proliferation giving rise to downstream recognitions and notions that then overrun the person.
notes: This formula should be read together with the preceding MN 18 lines. The repository keeps nidāna on its source-side sense here because the sentence is tracing what the later spread comes from. The difficult compound papañcasaññāsaṅkhā is governed separately so the whole line does not collapse into a single vague reference to mental proliferation.

example_phrases.pali: yaṁ papañceti tato nidānaṁ purisaṁ papañcasaññāsaṅkhā samudācaranti
example_phrases.translation: from what one proliferates about as the source, the recognitions and notions of proliferation sweep over a person
example_phrases.notes: Culminating line in the MN 18 sequence.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the line in which proliferation becomes the source for the later spread of papañcasaññāsaṅkhā.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use the full recorded rendering when this formula is translated directly.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Use the shorter alternate only when the source-side force of nidāna is already clear from the surrounding English. Do not flatten papañcasaññāsaṅkhā into generic mental proliferation here.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the culminating MN 18 line from collapsing the source-force of nidāna and the governed compound papañcasaññāsaṅkhā into a single vague paraphrase.
